blocks.body: Past Continuous: Questions and Short Answers — Was I...?, Was she...?, Were they...? and short answers.

{"level":"A2","intro":"In Past Continuous questions, the form was / were moves before the subject, and the main verb stays in -ing.","rules":[{"label":"Was I / he / she / it ...?","color":"emerald","text":"With I / he / she / it, start the question with was.","example":"Was she sleeping at 10 p.m.?"},{"label":"Were you / we / they ...?","color":"blue","text":"With you / we / they, use were before the subject.","example":"Were they waiting outside?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","text":"In short answers, repeat was / were or wasn't / weren't.","example":"Yes, I was. / No, they weren't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question models","intro":"In a question, the form of be comes before the subject.","items":[{"label":"I / he / she / it","title":"Was ... + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Was I talking too fast? / Was he sleeping?"},{"label":"you / we / they","title":"Were ... + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Were you working then? / Were they waiting for us?"},{"label":"Yes","title":"Yes, ... was / were.","subtitle":"Yes, I was. / Yes, they were."},{"label":"No","title":"No, ... wasn't / weren't.","subtitle":"No, she wasn't. / No, we weren't."}]}

{"title":"2. What these questions do","sections":[{"label":"Action at that moment","color":"emerald","description":"Ask what was happening at a particular moment in the past.","examples":[{"en":"Was he studying at 8 p.m.?","ua":"Ask about an action in progress at that time."},{"en":"Were they sleeping when you called?","ua":"Ask about the longer action in the past."}]},{"label":"Background for another event","color":"blue","description":"Ask about the longer action that was the background for another event.","examples":[{"en":"Was she cooking when you came in?","ua":"Ask about the background action."},{"en":"Were you driving when it started to rain?","ua":"One longer action and one event."}]},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","description":"Do not repeat the whole sentence. A short answer with was / were is enough.","examples":[{"en":"Was he working then? — Yes, he was.","ua":"Was he working then? — Yes, he was."},{"en":"Were they waiting? — No, they weren't.","ua":"Were they waiting? — No, they weren't."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Question and short answer","intro":"In the answer, repeat was / were, not the whole sentence.","rows":[{"en":"Was she sleeping at 10 p.m.?","ua":"Yes, she was. / No, she wasn't.","note":"was → was / wasn't"},{"en":"Were they waiting outside?","ua":"Yes, they were. / No, they weren't.","note":"were → were / weren't"},{"en":"Were you studying when I called?","ua":"Yes, I was. / No, I wasn't.","note":"With Were you...?, the answer is often I was / I wasn't."}],"warning":"Do not say Did she was sleeping? or Yes, they did."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding did to Past Continuous.","wrong":"❌ Did she was sleeping?","right":"✅ Was she sleeping?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Keeping was / were after the subject like in a statement.","wrong":"❌ They were waiting outside?","right":"✅ Were they waiting outside?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using did in a short answer.","wrong":"❌ Yes, they did.","right":"✅ Yes, they were."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Past Continuous question = was / were + subject + verb-ing.","With I / he / she / it, start with Was...?","With you / we / they, start with Were...?","In short answers, repeat was / were: Yes, I was. / No, they weren't."]}
